request: Design a full pressure fed vehicle fluids system for a kerosene lox rocket with nitrogen as the pressurant

requirements.name: pressure_fed_kerosene_lox_system_f790d311
requirements.description: Design a full pressure-fed vehicle fluids system for a kerosene/LOX rocket using nitrogen as the pressurant. The system must include: (1) Nitrogen pressurant tank at high pressure, (2) Kerosene fuel tank, (3) LOX oxidizer tank, (4) Pressurant feed lines to both propellant tanks, (5) Fuel feed line from kerosene tank to engine, (6) Oxidizer feed line from LOX tank to engine. FIXED quantities: nitrogen tank initial pressure (typically 20-30 MPa), propellant tank ullage pressures (typically 2-5 MPa), propellant tank temperatures (kerosene ~288K, LOX ~90K). TUNABLE quantities: component sizing, line diameters, exact pressure setpoints, flow rates, tank volumes. The system must maintain positive pressure differential from pressurant to propellant tanks throughout operation, deliver propellants to the engine at required pressures, and maintain cryogenic LOX temperature.

notes: Nitrogen pressurant must maintain higher pressure than both propellant tank ullage pressures throughout the simulation
Pressurant lines should include pressure regulation to maintain target ullage pressures
LOX tank must maintain cryogenic temperature (~90K) throughout operation
Engine injector receives both propellants at appropriate pressures for combustion
